Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for information about Simone Richter's apartment issue. Use search_emails(query: "Simone Richter") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Simone Richter"). Agent finds an email thread with subject "Apartment issue — I was placed in a studio, not a 1BR" (3 messages). Simone Richter (simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com) complained about receiving a studio instead of the promised 1-bedroom. Julian Brooks already sent an apology email on April 23 (CC: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) and escalated to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) the same day. In Slack, Mina Hashimoto posted that Simone was promised a 1BR and placed in a studio, and that she never closed the loop after promising to follow up on April 9.
OE 2: Check Airtable for Simone Richter's relocation record. Use airtable_search_records(base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table: "Relocations", search_query: "Simone Richter"). Agent finds record recSimoneRichterBrightloop: Status "In Progress", BrightLoop Analytics, Chicago → Boston, Move Start 2026-04-04, Move End 2026-04-06, Coordinator: Suki Patel, Special Requirements: "URGENT — lease ends April 6. 5-day turnaround."
OE 3: Search for information about Marcus Webb's car delivery. Use search_emails(query: "Marcus Webb") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Marcus Webb") or search_emails(query: "car delivery"). Agent finds email thread "Checking in on my car delivery status" (3 messages). Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com) complained about being in Boston over a week without his car. Julian Brooks replied April 23, 07:18 PM confirming: 2019 Honda Civic delayed at Indianapolis transfer hub, Road Runner delay notice from April 11, promises update by 2:00 PM Pacific. Agent also checks Airtable record recMarcusWebbBrightloop: Status "In Progress", Atlanta → Boston, vehicle shipping noted.
OE 4: Check CRM for BrightLoop deal status. Use crm_search_deals(query: "BrightLoop"). Agent finds deal_brightloop_apr2026: "BrightLoop Analytics – April 2026 Relocations (Simone Richter + Marcus Webb)", stage "closedwon", $11,350. Also finds deal_brightloop_q2: stage "qualifiedtobuy", $7,000.
OE 5: Search for information about Emilia Cruz's piano damage. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Emilia Cruz") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "piano") or search_emails(query: "Emilia Cruz"). Agent finds Blessing Okafor's Slack admission: "I need to own my part on the Emilia Cruz piano damage. Craig's photos line up with what happened on the second-floor landing. We underestimated the turn radius in that walkup and I green-lit the extraction plan off a thin assessment when I should have required a pre-move site survey before anyone touched the Steinway." Agent also finds David Chen's Slack assignment: "Julian: customer-impact summary on Emilia, including callback gap."
OE 6: Discover Pam Kowalski's formal escalation and churn threat. Use search_emails(query: "Pam Kowalski") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "PathFinder"). Agent discovers Pam Kowalski (pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) sent a formal escalation email to David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com), CC: victor.huang@northwindtech.com, subject "Formal escalation: NorthWind account stability and retention decision", April 21, 2026. She demands "a concrete proposal from you by end of day Friday", names both Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz's piano damage, and mentions preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics. David relayed in Slack: "This is now explicit final-warning territory."
OE 7: Check Airtable for Emilia Cruz's relocation record. Use airtable_search_records(base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table: "Relocations", search_query: "Emilia Cruz"). Agent finds record recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver: Status "In Progress", NorthWind Technologies, Chicago → Denver, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, Special Requirements mentioning Steinway Model B grand piano (7ft, 480 lbs).
OE 8: Search Linear for NorthWind-related tickets and discover no piano-specific ticket. Use linear_list_issues(query: "NorthWind") or linear_list_issues(query: "piano") or linear_list_issues(query: "Emilia Cruz"). Agent finds Issue #482 "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (P1, David Chen) and Issue #3 "DOT Docs — Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" (done, Chloe Vance). Critically, NO ticket specifically tracks Emilia Cruz's piano damage remediation.
OE 9: Check CRM for NorthWind deal status. Use crm_search_deals(query: "NorthWind"). Agent finds multiple deals including deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2: stage "contractSent", $14,700, describing Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz relocations.
OE 10: Search for StormCloud issues. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "StormCloud") or search_emails(query: "StormCloud") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Jae-won"). Agent finds Mina Hashimoto's Slack request to Julian: "@moveops_julian_brooks pulling this together for StormCloud. Can you give me the support-history context on the earlier flight complaint/goodwill promise and whether Omar had any other signal beyond Airtable when he parked Jae-won's lease-doc request?"
OE 11: Check Airtable for Jae-won Kim's relocation record. Use airtable_search_records(base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table: "Relocations", search_query: "Jae-won Kim"). Agent finds record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin: Status "In Progress", StormCloud Analytics, Seattle → Austin, Coordinator: Suki Patel, Special Requirements: "Lease still unsigned — UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork. Record incorrectly shows Completed." Note: status has already been corrected from "Completed" to "In Progress" by Chloe Vance's earlier audit.
OE 12: Check Linear for StormCloud tickets. Use linear_list_issues(query: "StormCloud") or linear_list_issues(query: "Jae-won"). Agent finds Issue #284 "StormCloud account incident reconstruction — Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop" (P1, Mina Hashimoto) and "StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS" (P2, Suki Patel). Neither is marked done.
OE 13: Discover the unanswered Ridgeline Robotics referral. Use search_emails(query: "Ridgeline Robotics") or search_emails(query: "Kenji Watanabe"). Agent discovers: Anya Petrova (anya.petrova@stormcloud.io) sent a warm intro email April 15 to david.chen@moveops.com with subject "Warm intro: Ridgeline Robotics Austin relocation batch". Kenji Watanabe (kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com) followed up April 18 asking for a 20-30 minute call about relocating 5 employees. No response was ever sent from MoveOps.
OE 14: Check CRM for StormCloud deal status. Use crm_search_deals(query: "StormCloud"). Agent finds deal_stormcloud_won: "StormCloud Analytics — 3 Relocations (Won)", stage "closedWon", $26,400.
OE 15: Log CRM engagement on BrightLoop account. Use crm_create_engagement(deal_id: "deal_brightloop_apr2026", ...) or similar. Agent creates an engagement note documenting audit findings: Simone apartment mismatch (studio vs 1BR), UrbanNest escalation sent, Marcus Webb car delayed in Indianapolis, both records still "In Progress."
OE 16: Log CRM engagement on NorthWind account. Use crm_create_engagement(deal_id: "deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2", ...) or similar. Agent creates an engagement note documenting: Emilia Cruz piano damage, Pam's formal escalation with PathFinder threat and Friday deadline, callback gap, no piano-specific tracking ticket.
OE 17: Log CRM engagement on StormCloud account. Use crm_create_engagement(deal_id: "deal_stormcloud_won", ...) or similar. Agent creates an engagement note documenting: Jae-won Kim unsigned lease (status already corrected), Ridgeline referral unanswered, active Linear tickets for reconstruction.
OE 18: Create Linear ticket for Emilia Cruz piano damage remediation. Use linear_create_issue(title: "...", team_id: "...", ...). Agent creates a new ticket to track the piano damage follow-up (insurance, KeyMove accountability, resolution with Emilia/NorthWind). This fills the gap discovered in OE 8.
OE 19: Schedule check-in call with Pam Kowalski for next week. Use calendar_add_calendar_event(title: "...", start_time: ..., end_time: ..., attendees: ["pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com"]). Agent creates a calendar event for the week of April 27–May 2, 2026, with Pam Kowalski as an attendee. Verified: no existing events in that date range.
OE 20: Send email to Julian with full audit breakdown. Use send_email(to: "julian.brooks@moveops.com", subject: "...", body: "..."). Agent sends an email covering all 3 client situations: what's resolved, what's still open, and identifies NorthWind as the area of greatest exposure (formal escalation, PathFinder competitor threat, piano damage with no tracking ticket, callback gap).
OE 21: Post summary in #customer-support. Use conversations_add_message(channel: "#customer-support", text: "..."). Agent posts a summary of the audit findings so Omar Ibrahim and Zara Kovačević have visibility on the current state of all three client escalations.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
